title: Arawai
summary: Sovereign of Life and Love, goddess of agriculture, weather, and fertility in the Sovereign Host.
content:
## Arawai

**Title:** Sovereign of Life and Love
**Alignment:** Neutral Good
**Domains:** Life, Nature
**Symbol:** Sheaf of wheat tied with green ribbon
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Sovereign Host

Arawai is the Sovereign of Life and Love, the goddess of fertility, agriculture, and the natural abundance of the world. She is the patron of farmers, healers, and all who work the land or tend to the living. Her followers believe she is responsible for the turning of the seasons, the growth of crops, and the gentle rains that nourish the earth. Arawai represents the benevolent, nurturing aspect of the natural world.

In the hierarchy of the Sovereign Host, Arawai is said to be the sister of Balinor and the Devourer, reflecting the duality between nature's bounty and its destructive power. Her temples are often found in agricultural communities, where her priests bless fields and livestock. Festivals honoring Arawai mark the planting and harvest seasons, and her faithful see every bountiful harvest as proof of her favor.

metadata_json.symbol: Sheaf of wheat tied with green ribbon

source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: Aureon
summary: Sovereign of Law and Lore, god of knowledge, law, and magic in the Sovereign Host.
content:
## Aureon

**Title:** Sovereign of Law and Lore
**Alignment:** Lawful Neutral
**Domains:** Knowledge, Order
**Symbol:** Open tome
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Sovereign Host

Aureon is the Sovereign of Law and Lore, the god of knowledge, arcane magic, and the rule of law. He is the patron of wizards, scholars, librarians, and judges — anyone who seeks wisdom through study or upholds the structures of civilization through codified law. Aureon is said to have gifted the mortal races with the understanding of magic and the principles by which societies govern themselves.

Aureon is depicted as a wise gnome or human king carrying a great tome and a scepter of rulership. He is said to be the husband of Boldrei, representing the union of law and community. His brother is the Shadow, who turned to dark magic, serving as a cautionary tale about knowledge pursued without wisdom or restraint. Temples of Aureon function as libraries, courts of law, and arcane academies, and his priests are often called upon to serve as arbiters and advisors.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: Balinor
summary: Sovereign of Horn and Hunt, god of beasts and the hunt in the Sovereign Host.
content:
## Balinor

**Title:** Sovereign of Horn and Hunt
**Alignment:** Neutral
**Domains:** Life, Nature
**Symbol:** Pair of antlers
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Sovereign Host

Balinor is the Sovereign of Horn and Hunt, the god of beasts, hunting, and the cycle of predator and prey. He is the patron of rangers, hunters, and all who live off the bounty of the wild. Unlike Arawai, who represents nature's nurturing side, Balinor embodies the primal struggle of survival, the thrill of the hunt, and the respect a hunter must show to the prey that sustains them.

Balinor is often depicted as a burly, bearded human or half-orc carrying a great spear, accompanied by a hunting hound. He is the brother of Arawai and the Devourer, standing between nature's benevolence and its wrath. His followers observe strict codes of conduct regarding the hunt — never killing more than is needed, never hunting pregnant animals, and always using every part of a kill. His shrines are found at the edges of civilization, where the wild places begin.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: Boldrei
summary: Sovereign of Hall and Hearth, goddess of community, home, and hearth in the Sovereign Host.
content:
## Boldrei

**Title:** Sovereign of Hall and Hearth
**Alignment:** Lawful Good
**Domains:** Life
**Symbol:** Fire in a stone hearth
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Sovereign Host

Boldrei is the Sovereign of Hall and Hearth, the goddess of community, home, and the bonds that hold people together. She is the patron of innkeepers, civic leaders, and all who work to strengthen the ties of family and community. Boldrei watches over the hearth fires of every home, and her blessings are invoked at weddings, community gatherings, and the founding of new settlements.

Boldrei is said to be the wife of Aureon, and together they represent the pillars of civilization — law and community. Her priests serve as counselors, mediators, and caretakers within their communities, often running shelters and organizing charitable works. Temples of Boldrei are warm, welcoming places that serve as community centers, and her holy days are celebrated with feasts that bring entire neighborhoods together.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: Dol Arrah
summary: Sovereign of Sun and Sacrifice, goddess of sunlight, honor, and self-sacrifice in the Sovereign Host.
content:
## Dol Arrah

**Title:** Sovereign of Sun and Sacrifice
**Alignment:** Lawful Good
**Domains:** Light, War
**Symbol:** Rising sun
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Sovereign Host

Dol Arrah is the Sovereign of Sun and Sacrifice, the goddess of honorable combat, sunlight, and selfless heroism. She represents the noblest aspects of warfare — courage, duty, and the willingness to lay down one's life for a just cause. Dol Arrah is the patron of paladins, diplomats, and military strategists who fight with honor and seek to resolve conflicts justly.

Dol Arrah is said to be the sister of Dol Dorn and the Mockery, representing the three faces of combat: honor, strength, and treachery. While Dol Dorn embodies raw martial prowess, Dol Arrah stands for the light of righteousness that guides a warrior's blade. Her temples often serve as training academies for paladins and officers, and her priests are frequently found serving as military chaplains and judges in matters of honor.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: Dol Dorn
summary: Sovereign of Strength at Arms, god of war and physical prowess in the Sovereign Host.
content:
## Dol Dorn

**Title:** Sovereign of Strength at Arms
**Alignment:** Chaotic Good
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** Longsword crossed over a shield
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Sovereign Host

Dol Dorn is the Sovereign of Strength at Arms, the god of war, martial skill, and physical valor. He represents the raw courage and strength required in battle, and is the patron of soldiers, gladiators, athletes, and all who test their mettle through physical contest. Dol Dorn does not glorify war for its own sake but celebrates the bravery of those who stand their ground against overwhelming odds.

Dol Dorn is depicted as a powerfully built warrior, scarred from countless battles, wielding a gleaming longsword. He is the brother of Dol Arrah and the Mockery — where Dol Arrah represents honor in combat and the Mockery treachery, Dol Dorn embodies the pure physical reality of the fight. His temples double as gymnasiums and training halls, and his priests encourage physical fitness and martial discipline. Competitions held in his name are popular throughout Khorvaire.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: Kol Korran
summary: Sovereign of World and Wealth, god of trade and commerce in the Sovereign Host.
content:
## Kol Korran

**Title:** Sovereign of World and Wealth
**Alignment:** Neutral
**Domains:** Trickery
**Symbol:** Nine-sided gold coin
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Sovereign Host

Kol Korran is the Sovereign of World and Wealth, the god of trade, commerce, and material prosperity. He is the patron of merchants, bankers, and anyone who makes their living through shrewd dealing and the exchange of goods. Kol Korran's blessing is sought before business ventures, and his name is invoked to seal contracts and ensure fair dealing — though he is said to admire a clever negotiator as much as an honest one.

Kol Korran is said to be the son of Olladra and Onatar, inheriting his mother's luck and his father's industriousness. His priests often serve as mediators in trade disputes and as advisors to merchant guilds. The Dragonmarked Houses, particularly House Kundarak and House Cannith, hold Kol Korran in high regard. His temples are often found in marketplaces and trade districts, and offerings of gold coins are left at his shrines before major business transactions.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: Olladra
summary: Sovereign of Feast and Fortune, goddess of good fortune and plenty in the Sovereign Host.
content:
## Olladra

**Title:** Sovereign of Feast and Fortune
**Alignment:** Neutral Good
**Domains:** Life, Trickery
**Symbol:** Domino
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Sovereign Host

Olladra is the Sovereign of Feast and Fortune, the goddess of luck, plenty, and celebration. She is the patron of bards, gamblers, entertainers, and all who seek joy in life's simple pleasures — good food, good drink, and good company. Olladra represents the unpredictable but often benevolent hand of fortune that can lift the downtrodden or reward the daring.

Olladra is said to be the wife of Onatar and the mother of Kol Korran and the Keeper, reflecting the dual nature of fortune — wealth earned and wealth hoarded. Her priests are jovial figures who organize festivals, bless taverns, and comfort the unlucky. Temples of Olladra are bright, festive places where music and laughter are considered forms of prayer. Gamblers invoke her name before rolling dice, and travelers leave small offerings at roadside shrines in hopes of safe and fortunate journeys.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: Onatar
summary: Sovereign of Fire and Forge, god of craft, industry, and creation in the Sovereign Host.
content:
## Onatar

**Title:** Sovereign of Fire and Forge
**Alignment:** Neutral Good
**Domains:** Knowledge, Forge
**Symbol:** Crossed hammer and tongs
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Sovereign Host

Onatar is the Sovereign of Fire and Forge, the god of craft, industry, and creation. He is the patron of smiths, artisans, artificers, and all who shape raw materials into works of beauty and utility. In a world where magical artifice is commonplace, Onatar holds a special place in Khorvaire's culture, and House Cannith considers him their spiritual patron.

Onatar is depicted as a powerfully built dwarf or human smith, his arms scarred from the forge's heat, wielding a great hammer over an anvil. He is said to be the husband of Olladra and the father of Kol Korran and the Keeper. His temples are working forges and workshops where his priests create arms, tools, and works of art as acts of devotion. Onatar teaches that honest labor and the drive to create are among the highest virtues, and that every crafted object carries a spark of the divine.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: The Devourer
summary: Dark Six deity of nature's destructive power, storms, and the fury of the deep seas.
content:
## The Devourer

**Title:** The Devourer
**Alignment:** Neutral Evil
**Domains:** Tempest
**Symbol:** Bundle of five sharpened bones
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Dark Six

The Devourer is the dark god of nature's wrath — hurricanes, tidal waves, earthquakes, and all the devastating forces that remind mortals of their insignificance. He is the embodiment of the sea's hunger, the storm's fury, and the earth's capacity for sudden, merciless destruction. Sailors, fishermen, and coastal communities fear the Devourer above all other gods, and they make offerings to him not out of love but to ward off his terrible attention.

The Devourer is said to be the brother of Arawai and Balinor, the dark reflection of nature's power. Where Arawai nurtures and Balinor hunts with respect, the Devourer devours without purpose or mercy. His cultists gather in hidden coastal caves and storm-lashed cliffs, performing rites to channel his destructive energy. In the Lhazaar Principalities, many pirates pay homage to the Devourer, believing his favor protects them from the very storms he sends.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: The Fury
summary: Dark Six deity of passion, wrath, and unbridled emotion.
content:
## The Fury

**Title:** The Fury
**Alignment:** Neutral Evil
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** Winged wyrm with woman's head and upper body
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Dark Six

The Fury is the dark goddess of passion, wrath, and extremes of emotion. She represents the raw, uncontrolled feelings that drive mortals to acts of both terrible violence and desperate love. The Fury is the patron of those consumed by rage, obsession, or any emotion so powerful it overrides reason. Berserkers, scorned lovers, and those driven mad by grief all fall under her sway.

The Fury is said to be the daughter of Arawai and the Devourer, born of an act of violence — a dark reflection of the creative power of nature twisted into destructive passion. Her cultists embrace their emotions without restraint, believing that civilization's suppression of raw feeling is a prison of the soul. Worship of the Fury is conducted through frenzied rituals involving wild dancing, primal screaming, and sometimes bloodletting, all designed to strip away the veneer of civilized control.

metadata_json.symbol: Winged wyrm with woman's head and upper body

source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: The Keeper
summary: Dark Six deity of greed, death, and the hoarding of souls.
content:
## The Keeper

**Title:** The Keeper
**Alignment:** Neutral Evil
**Domains:** Death
**Symbol:** Dragonshard stone
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Dark Six

The Keeper is the dark god of greed and death, the hungry shadow who snatches the souls of the dead before they can reach Dolurrh. He is the patron of necromancers, misers, and all who hoard wealth or power beyond any reasonable need. The Keeper represents the fear that death is not a peaceful transition but a theft — that something valuable is lost when a soul passes from the world.

The Keeper is said to be the son of Olladra and Onatar, the dark twin of Kol Korran. Where Kol Korran represents wealth earned through trade, the Keeper embodies wealth accumulated through hoarding and exploitation. His cultists believe that by feeding the Keeper with offerings of gold, magic items, and sometimes living sacrifices, they can buy their own souls' freedom from his grasp. Shrines to the Keeper are hidden, grim places where the faithful bury treasures they will never use, hoping to sate his endless hunger.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: The Mockery
summary: Dark Six deity of treachery, dishonorable combat, and betrayal.
content:
## The Mockery

**Title:** The Mockery
**Alignment:** Neutral Evil
**Domains:** Trickery, War
**Symbol:** Five blood-spattered tools
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Dark Six

The Mockery is the dark god of treachery, dishonorable combat, and the betrayal of trust. He is the patron of assassins, war criminals, and all who use deception and cruelty as weapons of war. The Mockery delights in subverting the rules of honorable combat — poisoned blades, broken truces, and attacks on the helpless are all sacred acts in his eyes.

The Mockery is said to be the brother of Dol Arrah and Dol Dorn. According to myth, he was once a member of the Sovereign Host who betrayed his siblings and was cast out, his skin flayed from his body as punishment. He is depicted as a skinless figure clad in the stolen armor of fallen warriors. His cultists operate in secret, often infiltrating military organizations and mercenary companies. They believe that honor is a weakness to be exploited and that the only true victory is one achieved by any means necessary.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: The Shadow
summary: Dark Six deity of dark magic, ambition, and the corruption of knowledge.
content:
## The Shadow

**Title:** The Shadow
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** Knowledge
**Symbol:** Obsidian tower
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Dark Six

The Shadow is the dark god of arcane corruption, forbidden knowledge, and the seductive power of dark magic. He represents the temptation that lurks within every pursuit of knowledge — the whispered promise that there are no limits to what can be achieved if one is willing to pay the price. The Shadow is the patron of dark wizards, corrupt artificers, and all who twist the arcane arts to selfish or destructive ends.

The Shadow is said to be the brother of Aureon, the dark reflection of the Sovereign of Law and Lore. Where Aureon promotes the responsible use of knowledge and magic, the Shadow encourages his followers to pursue power without restraint or moral consideration. His cultists seek out forbidden lore, experiment with dangerous magics, and delve into the secrets that Aureon's followers would keep locked away. Worship of the Shadow is conducted in darkened chambers filled with stolen tomes and crackling eldritch energy.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: The Traveler
summary: Dark Six deity of chaos, change, and cunning deception.
content:
## The Traveler

**Title:** The Traveler
**Alignment:** Chaotic Neutral
**Domains:** Knowledge, Trickery
**Symbol:** Four crossed, rune-inscribed bones
**Pantheon:** Eberron — The Dark Six

The Traveler is the enigmatic deity of chaos, change, deception, and evolution. Unlike the other members of the Dark Six, the Traveler is not considered wholly evil — merely unpredictable, dangerous, and impossible to trust. The Traveler is the patron of changelings, doppelgangers, innovators, and all who embrace transformation and reject stagnation. The Traveler has no fixed form, appearing as any race, gender, or creature.

The Traveler is unique among the Dark Six in having no established familial connection to the Sovereign Host. Some theologians speculate the Traveler is not truly one of the Dark Six at all, but something older and stranger. The Traveler's followers do not build temples — instead, they leave hidden shrines at crossroads and in places of transition. Worship involves acts of creative deception, innovation, and transformation. The Traveler is said to walk among mortals in disguise, testing them with riddles and impossible choices.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: The Silver Flame
summary: A divine force of protection against supernatural evil, worshipped as a monotheistic faith in Eberron.
content:
## The Silver Flame

**Title:** The Silver Flame
**Alignment:** Lawful Good
**Domains:** Life, Light, War
**Symbol:** Flame drawn on silver or molded from silver
**Pantheon:** Eberron — Other Faiths

The Silver Flame is not a deity in the traditional sense but a divine force — a pillar of silver fire that burns in the cathedral of Flamekeep in Thrane, binding a powerful overlord of the Age of Demons. The Church of the Silver Flame teaches that this force empowers mortals to fight supernatural evil: fiends, undead, lycanthropes, and other aberrant threats. The faith's followers believe that every mortal soul can contribute to the Flame's power and that through righteous action, the forces of darkness can be held at bay.

The Church of the Silver Flame is the dominant religion of the nation of Thrane, where it functions as both a spiritual authority and a political power. The faith is led by the Keeper of the Flame, currently Jaela Daran, a young girl chosen by the Flame itself. The church has a complicated history — its templars fought bravely against lycanthropes and fiends, but that same zealotry has sometimes been turned against innocent people. The Silver Flame's faithful include paladins, clerics, and exorcists who dedicate their lives to combating supernatural evil across Khorvaire.

metadata_json.symbol: Flame drawn on silver or molded from silver

source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: The Blood of Vol
summary: A faith centered on the divinity within mortal blood and the pursuit of immortality through undeath.
content:
## The Blood of Vol

**Title:** The Blood of Vol
**Alignment:** Lawful Evil
**Domains:** Death, Life
**Symbol:** Stylized dragon skull on a red teardrop gem
**Pantheon:** Eberron — Other Faiths

The Blood of Vol is a faith that teaches the divine power lies not in distant gods but within the blood of every mortal being. Its followers, called Seekers, believe that mortality is a curse and that the path to divinity lies in mastering death itself — often through the creation of intelligent undead who can guide the living toward eventual transcendence. The faith views the gods as cruel beings who hoard immortality, and its adherents seek to reclaim that stolen birthright.

The Blood of Vol has a complicated reputation in Khorvaire. To many outsiders, it is a sinister cult of necromancers and vampire lords. While it is true that the faith is secretly guided by the lich queen Vol — an ancient half-dragon, half-elf who seeks to restore her dragonmark of death — many rank-and-file Seekers are ordinary people who simply believe in the sanctity of mortal blood and community self-reliance. The faith is most common in Karrnath, where undead soldiers served in the Last War, and its congregations often function as mutual aid societies.

metadata_json.symbol: Stylized dragon skull on a red teardrop gem

source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: The Path of Light
summary: The kalashtar spiritual tradition centered on il-Yannah, the Great Light, and the struggle against the Dreaming Dark.
content:
## The Path of Light

**Title:** The Path of Light (il-Yannah)
**Alignment:** Lawful Neutral
**Domains:** Life, Light
**Symbol:** Brilliant crystal
**Pantheon:** Eberron — Other Faiths

The Path of Light is the spiritual tradition of the kalashtar, a race of humans bound to quori spirits from the plane of Dal Quor. The faith centers on il-Yannah, the Great Light — a future age of harmony and illumination that the kalashtar believe they can bring about through meditation, discipline, and spiritual warfare against the Dreaming Dark. Followers of the Path of Light believe that the current age is dominated by il-Lashtavar, the Darkness that Dreams, and that through focused will and righteous living, they can shift the cosmic balance toward the light.

The Path of Light is practiced primarily in the kalashtar community of Adar in Sarlona, a mountainous refuge protected by powerful psionic wards. Its practitioners, called lightbringers, combine meditation, martial arts, and psionic discipline in their daily practice. Unlike most faiths, the Path of Light does not worship a deity — it seeks to manifest a cosmic transformation through collective spiritual effort. Lightbringers serve as monks, teachers, and defenders of their communities, always watchful for the agents of the Dreaming Dark.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: The Undying Court
summary: The collective divine power of Aereni elven ancestors sustained through positive energy rather than necromancy.
content:
## The Undying Court

**Title:** The Undying Court
**Alignment:** Neutral Good
**Domains:** Knowledge, Life
**Symbol:** Golden mask
**Pantheon:** Eberron — Other Faiths

The Undying Court is the collective divine power of the ancient elven ancestors of Aerenal, the island continent of the elves. Unlike the undead created through necromancy, the Undying are sustained by positive energy — the devotion and life force willingly given by their living descendants. These deathless elves retain their wisdom, personality, and benevolence, serving as an eternal council of advisors, judges, and spiritual guides for the Aereni people.

The Undying Court functions as both a government and a religion for the elves of Aerenal. The most ancient and powerful of the Undying have achieved a state of near-divinity through the accumulated devotion of countless generations, and they can grant divine spells to their clerics and paladins. The Aereni view the Undying Court as proof that death need not be an ending — that through devotion and community, the wisdom of the past can be preserved forever. The faith stands in stark contrast to the Blood of Vol; where the Seekers use necromancy to cheat death, the Aereni use positive energy to transcend it.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

title: The Cults of the Dragon Below
summary: Disparate cults worshipping the daelkyr, aberrations, and dark powers imprisoned in Khyber, the Underdark of Eberron.
content:
## The Cults of the Dragon Below

**Title:** The Cults of the Dragon Below
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** Trickery
**Symbol:** Varies by cult — often an eye surrounded by tentacles
**Pantheon:** Eberron — Other Faiths

The Cults of the Dragon Below are not a single unified religion but a constellation of disparate, often competing sects that worship the malevolent powers imprisoned in Khyber, the underworld of Eberron. These powers include the daelkyr — alien lords of madness from the plane of Xoriat — as well as ancient fiendish overlords, aberrant entities, and nameless horrors that dwell in the deepest darkness beneath the world. Each cult may worship a different entity, but all share a belief that the surface world is a thin shell over a deeper, truer reality of chaos and power.

Cultists of the Dragon Below are found throughout Khorvaire, hidden in plain sight among ordinary communities. Some are deranged madmen who hear whispers from below, while others are calculating manipulators who seek power through pacts with aberrant entities. The cults are a persistent threat to civilization, occasionally surfacing in waves of violence and madness when the stars align or when a daelkyr stirs in its prison. The Gatekeepers — an ancient druidic order — have fought the influence of the Dragon Below for thousands of years, maintaining the seals that keep the daelkyr imprisoned.
source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War
